---
title: "Quantization Cliff"
subtitle: "Dial layer precisions. Ship within budget."
description: "A browser mini-game from MLSys Playground. Six neural network layers, four precision levels, three deploys to ship within the bit budget. Teaches mixed-precision quantization."
page-layout: article
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <link rel="stylesheet" href="/assets/games/common.css">
          <script src="/assets/games/common.js" defer></script>
          <script src="/assets/games/quantization.js" defer></script>
---

```{=html}
<div class="mlsp-game-container" role="region" aria-label="Quantization Cliff mini-game">
  <canvas id="mlsp-canvas" class="mlsp-game-canvas" width="680" height="460"></canvas>
  <div class="mlsp-game-hud">
    <span class="mlsp-score">bits <span id="mlsp-bits">192</span> / <span id="mlsp-budget">96</span> · deploys <span id="mlsp-deploys">3</span></span>
    <span>click layer to cycle precision · <kbd>space</kbd> to deploy · <kbd>R</kbd> retry</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div id="mlsp-aha-slot"></div>

<script>
(function mountQuant(){
  function boot() {
    if (!window.MLSP || !MLSP.games || !MLSP.games.quantization) return setTimeout(boot, 30);
    var canvas = document.getElementById("mlsp-canvas");
    var $bits    = document.getElementById("mlsp-bits");
    var $budget  = document.getElementById("mlsp-budget");
    var $deploys = document.getElementById("mlsp-deploys");
    var ahaSlot  = document.getElementById("mlsp-aha-slot");
    var api = MLSP.games.quantization(canvas, {
      onScoreChange: function(s) {
        $bits.textContent = s.bitsUsed;
        $budget.textContent = s.budget;
        $deploys.textContent = s.deploysLeft;
      },
      onGameOver: function() {
        MLSP.showAhaCard(ahaSlot, api.ahaLabel, api.ahaText);
      },
      onRetry: function() { window.location.reload(); }
    });
  }
  boot();
})();
</script>
```

## How it works

You're shipping a quantized model. Six layers, each starting at fp32 (32 bits per weight). You have **96 bits total** to spend — half the original. Click a layer to cycle its precision: fp32 → fp16 → int8 → int4 → fp32.

Press **deploy** to push the model and see the resulting accuracy. You get **3 deploys per run**. Hit **85% accuracy** within budget and you ship.

The catch: **layer sensitivity is hidden and uneven**. First and last layers (embeddings, output heads) tend to hate aggressive quantization. Middle layers usually tolerate int8 or int4 fine. You'll learn which layers are robust by deploying and watching where the cliff drops.

## Why this matters

Real production quantization (HAQ, SmoothQuant, AWQ) is exactly this problem — choosing precision per layer under a total bit budget — but solved by reinforcement learning instead of intuition. Embeddings and output heads at low precision destroy accuracy; middle layers are surprisingly robust. The reason your phone can run a 7B-parameter model is that someone made these calls correctly.

---

*Part of [MLSys Playground](/games/).*
